item: ripple-resolver-verify: omnia/ripple.iii vec_u64 handle exhaustion + silent witness-full drop
reachability_fact: ripple.iii:188 `let seeds : u64 = vec_u64_new(arena_id, 4u64)` -- consumes one of 16 global VEC64_LIVE slots (vec.iii:178 `const VEC_U64_MAX : u32 = 16u32`). ripple.iii never calls `vec_u64_drop(seeds)`. sid_transitive_closure (crystal_deps.iii:134) allocates its own result vec (`let result : u64 = vec_u64_new(arena_id, SID_MAX_CLOSURE)`); this handle is returned as closure_v to ripple_analyze (ripple.iii:191), which also never drops it. Total leak = 2 VEC64_LIVE slots per ripple_analyze call. After 8 calls, vec_u64_new returns VEC_INVALID(0), seeds==0 -> ripple_analyze returns RIPPLE_INVALID(0). Confirmed by grep: zero callers of vec_u64_drop exist anywhere in STDLIB/iii/omnia/ (grep vec_u64_drop -> only definition at vec.iii:301). Second defect: ripple_execute (ripple.iii:296-312) loops `while i < n` calling witness_append per edge; n=crystal_edges_count(plan_id) is u32 uncapped vs WITNESS_MAX_ENTRIES=1024 (witness.iii:24); witness_append return value is discarded (no `if` guard); partial witness coverage silently passes ripple_verify since it checks crystal_verify per edge, not witness presence. Resolver side: REFUTED -- resolver_memo uses a flat 4096-slot array (no handle table), no exhaustion possible.
maximal_design: Three-part maximal fix to omnia/ripple.iii: (1) Add `extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn vec_u64_drop(id: u64) -> i32 from "vec.iii"` -- ripple.iii already imports vec_u64_new/push/count/at but is missing the drop extern. (2) In ripple_analyze: after `sid_transitive_closure` returns and the seeds vec is fully consumed, call `vec_u64_drop(seeds)`. After the plan edges loop that consumes closure_v, call `vec_u64_drop(closure_v)`. This restores both slots per call, making the function slot-neutral and correct for unbounded invocation sequences. (3) Add constant RIPPLE_E_WITNESS_FULL : i32 = -5i32. In ripple_execute, before the strict-mode witness loop, compute remaining_witness = WITNESS_MAX_ENTRIES - witness_count(); if mode==STRICT and n > remaining_witness, return RIPPLE_INVALID immediately (and set RIPPLE_LAST_ERR = RIPPLE_E_WITNESS_FULL). This turns a silent partial-witness defect into a loud typed failure. The resolve-side (resolver_memo, resolver_replay) is correct by construction and needs no change.

code_changes.anchor: extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn vec_u64_count(id: u64) -> u64 from "vec.iii"
code_changes.new_code:
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn vec_u64_count(id: u64) -> u64 from "vec.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn vec_u64_drop(id: u64) -> i32 from "vec.iii"

const RIPPLE_E_K_UNDERFLOW   : i32 = -4i32
const RIPPLE_E_WITNESS_FULL  : i32 = -5i32

code_changes.anchor:
let closure_v : u64 = sid_transitive_closure(arena_id, seeds)
    if closure_v == 0u64 { return RIPPLE_INVALID }

code_changes.new_code:
/* For each edge of plan: emit a witness record (strict mode only). */
    let mut i : u32 = 0u32

kat:
Corpus: `1000_ripple_handle_exhaust_guard.iii`

arm 1 (NEGATIVE -- proves fix fires on exhaustion path): calls `ripple_analyze` 9 times in the same arena on the same `VEC64_LIVE` singleton. Pre-fix: call 9 returns 0 (VEC64_LIVE full -- 2 slots leaked per call, 16 slots / 2 = 8 calls before full). Post-fix: each call drops seeds + closure_v (net 0 slot consumption) -> call 9 returns a valid non-zero plan_id. Gate: `if plan == 0u64 { return 11u64 }` on iterations 1..9 -- any exhaustion is caught.

arm 2 (NEGATIVE -- proves witness-headroom guard fires): fills witness table to 1020 entries via 1-edge strict ripple_execute calls, then attempts a 5-edge strict ripple_execute (needs 5 witness slots; only 4 free). Pre-fix: returns non-zero result with 4 witnesses written, 1 silently dropped. Post-fix: returns RIPPLE_INVALID (0). Gate: `if res5 != 0u64 { return 40u64 }` proves the pre-flight check fires.
